mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL signals rising inflation expectations that compress real equity returns through higher required risk premia, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack triggers downward earnings revisions and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS and UNRATE tighten financial conditions and reduce disposable income, jointly suppressing equity valuations and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS accelerates wage inflation fears and Fed tightening only when the funds rate is already elevated, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, increasing PAYEMS is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening residential investment and construction employment, reducing aggregate demand expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls by more than 0.5% month-over-month, it signals contraction in manufacturing output that drags on corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances when INDPRO declines by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during tightening episodes signal that rising demand is met with restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
